---
title: "The Grammar of Graphics Assembles"
subtitle: |
  [R]{.fragment fragment-index="2"}[, Python]{.fragment fragment-index="3"}[, SQL...]{.fragment fragment-index="4"} [and Now]{.fragment fragment-index="5"} [TYPST]{.cover-boom .fragment fragment-index="6"}
author:
  - name:
      given: "Mickaël"
      family: "CANOUIL"
    degrees: "Ph.D."
    url: "https://mickael.canouil.fr"
date: 2026-06-30
logo: assets/images/logo-stacked.svg
format:
  comic-revealjs:
    theme:
      - ./assets/stylesheets/custom.scss
    revealjs-plugins:
      - a11y
engine: markdown
filters:
  - typst-render
  - code-window
  - cascade
extensions:
  code-window:
    style: default
  typst-render:
    preamble: assets/typst/_preamble.typ
    format: svg
    align: center
    output-directory: ./assets/typst-render
    font-path: ./assets/fonts
    background: "#fdf6e3"
    foreground: "#0b0b0b"
    cache: false
---

# Issue 01: Origin Story {.section}

Every hero starts with a problem nobody else will fix.

## The Dark Past {.panel}

- [Different fonts between prose and figure.]{.fragment fragment-index="1" .comic-pop}
- [Different colour palettes: slide theme vs. plot theme.]{.fragment fragment-index="2" .comic-pop}
- [Two render passes, document and figure compiled separately.]{.fragment fragment-index="3" .comic-pop}
- [No shared layout, so figure margins are guesswork.]{.fragment fragment-index="4" .comic-pop}

::: {.columns .fragment fragment-index="5"}
::: {.column width="50%"}

**Gribouille**

```{.typst style="font-size: 0.9em;"}
#figure(
  plot(data: penguins, ...),
  caption: [Figure 1.],
)
```

:::
::: {.column width="50%"}

**R / Python + import**

```r
ggplot(penguins) + ...
ggsave("fig.svg")
```

```typst
#image("fig.svg")
```

:::
:::

::: {.caption .fragment}
The scars every figure carried, workflow after workflow.
:::

---

::: {.shout .fragment}
Never again.  
What if one engine compiled the figure and the prose?
:::

## The Spark {.panel}

::: {.narration .fragment}
Before the powers, the ordinary day job.
:::

::: {.thought .fragment}
With **Typst Render** I can render any Typst content straight into Quarto.

But, why do I still need to use R or Python to draw a plot?
:::

---

- [I wanted a plot from a **CSV**: no kernel, no session, no heavy dependencies.]{.fragment fragment-index="1" .comic-pop}
- [Typst was already compiling diagrams for me. Why not the plot too?]{.fragment fragment-index="2" .comic-pop}

::: {.thought .fragment fragment-index="3"}
[A grammar of graphics...]{.fragment fragment-index="3"} [inside a database?]{.fragment fragment-index="4"}

[Why not inside a document compiler.]{.fragment fragment-index="5"}
:::

::: {.fragment fragment-index="4"}
Last year, **ggsql** brought the grammar into SQL.
:::

::: {.caption .fragment}
Gribouille was born of necessity, not ambition.
:::

# Issue 02: The Graphing API {.section}

Wilkinson's grammar, native to Typst.

## data + aes() {.panel}

```typst
#import "@preview/gribouille:0.4.1": *
```

```{typst}
//| echo: true
//| label: step1
//| width: 14cm
//| height: 9cm
//| code-line-numbers: "2-3"
//| alt: Empty plot frame with bill length on the x-axis and bill depth on the y-axis — the coordinate system is established before any layer is added.
#plot(
  data: penguins,
  mapping: aes(x: "bill-len", y: "bill-dep"),
  layers: (geom-blank(),),
  labels: labels(x: "Bill Length (mm)", y: "Bill Depth (mm)"),
  theme: theme-comic(),
  width: auto,
  height: auto,
)
```

::: {.caption .fragment style="font-size: 0.9em;"}
One line to import.
No R session, no Python environment.  
It is a Typst Universe package.
:::

## + geom-point() {.panel}

```typst
layers: (geom-point(size: 2pt),),
```

```{typst}
//| echo: false
//| label: step2
//| width: 14cm
//| height: 9cm
//| alt: "Scatter plot of bill length vs bill depth for 344 penguins, all marks in a single ink colour on a cream panel with a bold border."
#plot(
  data: penguins,
  mapping: aes(x: "bill-len", y: "bill-dep"),
  layers: (geom-point(size: 2pt),),
  labels: labels(x: "Bill Length (mm)", y: "Bill Depth (mm)"),
  theme: theme-comic(),
  width: auto,
  height: auto,
)
```

## + aes(fill = "species") {.panel}

```typst
mapping: aes(x: "bill-len", y: "bill-dep", fill: "species"),
scales: (scale-fill-discrete(palette: comic-colours),),
```

```{typst}
//| echo: false
//| label: step3
//| width: 14cm
//| height: 9cm
//| alt: "Scatter plot of bill length vs bill depth coloured by species: Adelie in red, Chinstrap in blue, Gentoo in yellow, with a legend."
#plot(
  data: penguins,
  mapping: aes(x: "bill-len", y: "bill-dep", fill: "species"),
  layers: (geom-point(size: 2pt, colour: black, stroke: 0.5pt),),
  scales: (
    scale-fill-discrete(
      limits: ("Adelie", "Chinstrap", "Gentoo"),
      palette: comic-colours,
    ),
  ),
  labels: labels(x: "Bill Length (mm)", y: "Bill Depth (mm)", fill: "Species"),
  theme: theme-comic(),
  width: auto,
  height: auto,
)
```

## + facet-wrap("species") {.panel}

```typst
facet: facet-wrap("species"),
```

```{typst}
//| echo: false
//| label: step4
//| width: 14cm
//| height: 9cm
//| alt: "Scatter plot faceted by species into three panels — Adelie, Chinstrap, Gentoo — each framed with a bold border and a yellow strip header."
#plot(
  data: penguins,
  mapping: aes(x: "bill-len", y: "bill-dep", fill: "species"),
  layers: (geom-point(size: 2pt, colour: black, stroke: 0.5pt),),
  facet: facet-wrap("species"),
  scales: (
    scale-fill-discrete(
      limits: ("Adelie", "Chinstrap", "Gentoo"),
      palette: comic-colours,
    ),
  ),
  guides: guides(fill: none),
  labels: labels(x: "Bill Length (mm)", y: "Bill Depth (mm)"),
  theme: theme-comic(),
  width: auto,
  height: auto,
)
```

## compose() {.panel}

```typst
compose(
  defer(plot, ...)),
  defer(plot, ...)),
  columns: 2,
  labels: labels(title: comic-title("Palmer Penguins: Morphometrics")),
  collect: ("fill",),
)
```

```{typst}
//| echo: false
//| label: compose
//| alt: "Two-panel composition: bill length vs bill depth on the left, flipper length vs body mass on the right, with a shared species colour legend."
//| width: 16cm
//| height: 9cm
#let my-plot(x, y, xlab, ylab) = {
  defer(plot,
    data: penguins,
    mapping: aes(x: x, y: y, fill: "species"),
    layers: (geom-point(size: 2pt, colour: black, stroke: 0.5pt),),
    scales: (scale-fill-discrete(limits: ("Adelie", "Chinstrap", "Gentoo"), palette: comic-colours,),),
    labels: labels(x: xlab, y: ylab, fill: "Species"),
    theme: theme-comic()
  )
}
#compose(
  my-plot("bill-len", "bill-dep", "Bill Length (mm)", "Bill Depth (mm)",),
  my-plot("flipper-len", "body-mass", "Flipper Length (mm)", "Body Mass (g)",),
  columns: 2,
  labels: labels(title: comic-title("Palmer Penguins: Morphometrics")),
  collect: ("fill",),
  width: auto,
  height: auto,
)
```

::: {.caption .fragment}
Typst has no lazy evaluation, so `defer()` captures the plot spec without rendering it yet.
:::

## annotate("typst", ...) {.panel}

```typst
annotate(
  "typst", ...,
  label: [#box(inset: 8pt, fill: rgb("#ffd60a"), stroke: 2pt + rgb("#0b0b0b"))[_Typical_ Chinstrap]],
  anchor: "west",
  size: 14pt,
  clip: false
),
```

```{typst}
//| echo: false
//| label: annotate
//| width: 14cm
//| height: 9cm
//| alt: "Scatter plot with a vertical red reference line at bill length 45 mm and an italic Typst annotation 'typical Chinstrap' placed near the cluster."
#plot(
  data: penguins,
  mapping: aes(x: "bill-len", y: "bill-dep", fill: "species"),
  layers: (
    geom-point(size: 2pt, colour: black, stroke: 0.5pt),
    annotate(
      "typst",
      x: 50.0,
      y: 13.5,
      label: [#box(inset: 8pt, fill: rgb("#ffd60a"), stroke: 2pt + rgb("#0b0b0b"))[_Typical_ Chinstrap]],
      anchor: "west",
      size: 14pt,
      clip: false
    ),
  ),
  scales: (
    scale-colour-discrete(
      limits: ("Adelie", "Chinstrap", "Gentoo"),
      palette: comic-colours,
    ),
  ),
  labels: labels(x: "Bill Length (mm)", y: "Bill Depth (mm)", colour: "Species"),
  theme: theme-comic(),
  width: auto,
  height: auto,
)
```

::: {.caption .fragment style="font-size: 0.9em;"}
The annotation is native Typst content, nothing extra to use.
:::

# Issue 03: Faithful, and Not {.section}

Same grammar, different runtime.

## Adherence vs. Departure {.panel}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}

**Faithful to ggplot2**

- [`aes()` mapping.]{.fragment .comic-pop}
- [Layered `geom*` / `stat*`.]{.fragment .comic-pop}
- [`scale*`, `facet*`, `coord*`.]{.fragment .comic-pop}
- [`guides()`, `theme*`.]{.fragment .comic-pop}
- [Layers stack in draw order.]{.fragment .comic-pop}

:::
::: {.column width="50%"}

**Departs by necessity**

- [kebab-case: `geom-point`, `stat-identity`.]{.fragment .comic-pop}
- [No `+` operator: one `plot()` call with named args (`layers`, `scales`, `facet`).]{.fragment .comic-pop}
- [Marker body is `fill`, outline is `colour`.]{.fragment .comic-pop}
- [`labels()` not `labs()`; avoid abbreviations.]{.fragment .comic-pop}

:::
:::

::: {.caption .fragment}
The vocabulary is stable. The question was never "what should the API look like?" It was "how do I wire it to Typst?"
:::

## Payoff: One Engine {.panel}

::: {.narration}
The figure stops being a tourist.
:::

In a native Typst document, the figure inherits the document's font, foreground, and background.  
Switch your document theme and the figures follow.

::: {.callout-tip collapse="false" title="The integration an SVG import can never close"}
Figure and prose are compiled by the **same engine**, in one pass.  
Annotate, caption, and cross-reference the plot like any other element.
:::

## {.explosion}

Same Engine!

{{< boom "Typst" colour=red bottom=20% right=2% rotate=-8 size=1.4em fragment=burst >}}

# Issue 04: Built With AI, 2026 {.section}

Porting a grammar with agentic tools.

## Timeline {.panel}

::: {.narration}
Manual first. AI later, once it was safe to.
:::

::: {.columns style="font-size: 0.85em;"}
::: {.column}

**Plan, then hand-build**

- Grammar and theme API design.
- `cetz` as the drawing layer.
- Core written and reviewed by hand.
- Tests, snapshots, docs, until the architecture was safe to automate on.

:::
::: {.column}

**Then accelerate**

- LLMs with proper guardrails.
- Heavy Human review.
- v0.1.0: May 20.
- v0.3.0: June 14.
- v0.4.0: June 22.
- v0.4.1: June 23 (current).

:::
:::

{{< boom "Really!" colour=red bottom=35% right=2% rotate=-8 size=1.4em fragment=burst >}}
{{< bam "v0.4.1" colour=yellow bottom=10% right=15% rotate=10 size=1.3em fragment=pop >}}

## Earn the Right to Automate {.panel}

::: {layout-ncol=3}

::: {.fragment .comic-pop fragment-index="1"}
**1. Hand-build**

Core grammar coded, edited, and reviewed manually.
No AI on an unstable foundation.
:::

::: {.fragment .comic-pop fragment-index="2"}
**2. Harden**

Tests, snapshots, documentation: the guardrails that make change safe.
:::

::: {.fragment .comic-pop fragment-index="3"}
**3. Accelerate**

Only then bring in LLMs, to speed up well-bounded work.
:::

:::

::: {.callout-note .fragment collapse="false" title="What carried, what didn't"}
Stable vocabulary ported cleanly, a good LLM target.  
AI stumbled on Typst's no-lazy-eval model, `cetz` primitives, and scale/stat edge cases.  
Human review caught it.
:::

{{< bam "manual" colour=blue top=-4%% left=19% rotate=-8 size=1.1em fragment=pop index=1 >}}
{{< boom "guardrails" colour=red top=-10% right=-5% rotate=6 size=1.1em fragment=pop index=3 >}}

# Issue 05: Where It Stands {.section}

{{< boom "v0.4.1" colour=red top=60% right=18% rotate=-8 size=1.6em fragment=burst >}}

## Rising Out of the Shadows {data-comic-fit="false" background-color="#0a132e"}

```{typst}
//| echo: false
//| width: 16cm
//| height: 18cm
//| file: "assets/star-history/star-history.typ"
//| output-filename: "star-history.svg"
//| alt: "Midnight-sky line chart of Gribouille's cumulative GitHub stars per day from April to June 2026, titled 'Gribouille, a Rising Star'. A luminous gold step trail rises from zero across a long flat run labelled 'Quietly built in private', lifts at a gold dashed marker labelled 'Made public 17th of May', then climbs through dashed release markers to a final amber spike annotated with a one-day jump."
```

## What Ships, What Doesn't {.panel}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}

- [**Geoms:** point, line, bar, col, area, boxplot, ribbon, histogram, contour, hex, tile, text, smooth, rug ...]{.fragment .comic-pop}
- [**Scales:** continuous, discrete, manual; brewer, viridis, okabe-ito, gradient; size, alpha, shape ...]{.fragment .comic-pop}
- [**Themes:** grey, minimal, classic, bw, void, linedraw, light, dark.]{.fragment .comic-pop}

:::
::: {.column width="45%"}

::: {.callout-warning .fragment collapse="false" title="Not there (_yet_)"}

- **Static output only.**
- **Performance at scale** not benchmarked.
- **Not all geoms/stats ported:** maps and complex stat pipelines (WASM possible).

:::

:::
:::

## Where to Help {.panel}

Feedbacks are welcome:

- [Bug reports: the test suite is young, edge cases are still being found as I use Gribouille, *e.g.*, [`m.canouil.dev/tidytuesday`](https://m.canouil.dev/tidytuesday/)]{.fragment .comic-pop}
- [Documentation examples: a lot of dummy examples, but a few "real" examples.]{.fragment .comic-pop}

::: {.caption .fragment}
Gribouille is an unfunded spare-time project, and parts of the API are still settling.  
[Pull requests are not being accepted for now.]{style="text-decoration: underline;"}
:::

## Links {.panel}

::: {.narration}
Gribouille is a scribble today.  
The grammar is stable enough to build on.
:::

- [**Typst Universe** — `@preview/gribouille:0.4.1`]{.fragment .comic-pop fragment-index="1"}
- [**Docs** — [`m.canouil.dev/gribouille`](https://m.canouil.dev/gribouille)]{.fragment .comic-pop fragment-index="3"}
- [**GitHub** — [`github.com/mcanouil/gribouille`](https://github.com/mcanouil/gribouille)]{.fragment .comic-pop fragment-index="4"}
- [**This talk** — [`https://m.canouil.dev/2026-ggplot2-extenders-gribouille/`](https://m.canouil.dev/2026-ggplot2-extenders-gribouille/)]{.fragment .comic-pop fragment-index="5"}
- [**TidyTuesday** — [`m.canouil.dev/tidytuesday`](https://m.canouil.dev/tidytuesday/)]{.fragment .comic-pop fragment-index="6"}

{{< bam "v0.4.1" colour=yellow bottom=-15% right=25% rotate=12 size=2em fragment=burst index=1 >}}

::: {.caption .fragment fragment-index="7"}
Thank you.
:::
